title: Statistics for Linguistics Network
text:
The Statistics for Linguistics Network is a community of researchers with an interest in statistical theory, including statistical concepts and methods as they pertain to language research.
The network provides a forum for researchers interested in the development, evaluation, and application of statistical approaches to language research, the philosophy of statistics, the implications of statistical assumptions and analytical choices for linguistic theory and empirical inference, and statistics education in linguistics.

title: Statistics for Linguistics Network
text:
The Statistics for Linguistics Network is a community of researchers with an interest in statistical theory, including statistical concepts and methods as they pertain to language research.
The network provides a forum for researchers interested in the development, evaluation, and application of statistical approaches to language research, the philosophy of statistics, the implications of statistical assumptions and analytical choices for linguistic theory and empirical inference, and statistics education in linguistics.

title: Statistics for Linguistics Network
text:
Aim
The aim of the network is to strengthen the role of statistical thinking in language research by fostering a community engaged with the conceptual foundations, methods, and implications of statistical practice.

title: Statistics for Linguistics Network
text:
Mission
The mission of the Statistics for Linguistics Network is to support rigorous, transparent, and theoretically informed approaches to statistical inference, and to connect researchers working on conceptual, methodological, and philosophical aspects of statistics as they relate to language research.

For more info, get in touch with Stefano Coretta at s.coretta at ed.ac.uk.
